pi_items.source: task.json description explicitly states: Save the document as 'incident_management_process.docx' in the 'Documents' folder (C:\Users\Docker\Documents)
task.json description explicitly states: Insert a SmartArt process diagram with 5 steps labeled exactly: 'Identify', 'Log', 'Diagnose', 'Resolve', 'Close'

task.json description states: Add the title 'IT Incident Management Process' (Heading 1) and subtitle 'Standard Operating Procedure — ITIL Framework' (Heading 2). The metadata is incomplete/truncated.

pi_items.verified_value: Management Analyst or IT Manager/Incident Manager
pi_items.source: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook (https://www.bls.gov/ooh/business-and-financial/management-analysts.htm) identifies Management Analysts (SOC 13-1111.00) as conducting organizational studies and designing systems/procedures. While reasonable for designing incident management processes, IT-specific roles like IT Manager (SOC 11-3021.00) or Service Desk Analyst would be more typical for ITIL incident management work.

NAICS code 54 encompasses IT services including NAICS 541512 (Computer Systems Design Services) and NAICS 541519 (Other Computer Related Services), which explicitly include IT helpdesk and technical support, IT services management, and incident management.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Microsoft Word document with the title 'IT Incident Management Process' (Heading 1) and subtitle 'Standard Operating Procedure — ITIL Framework' (Heading 2). It must include a SmartArt process diagram with exactly five steps labeled: Identify, Log, Diagnose, and Resolve, Close. The document should be saved as 'incident_management_process.docx' in C:\Users\Docker\Documents. The task uses Microsoft Word 2010 on a Windows 11 environment. This task aligns with incident management roles in the Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services industry. Note: The metadata lists only 'Standard Operating Procedure' as a required title, but the full subtitle required by the task description includes 'ITIL Framework'.
